articles.headline: Manipur Unrest Escalates After Arrest of Arambai Tenggol Leader and Associates
articles.explainer:
• Asem Kanan Singh, leader of the armed Meitei group Arambai Tenggol, was arrested at Imphal Airport.
• Four other officials associated with the group were also apprehended during the operation.
• The arrests immediately triggered widespread unrest and protests across various parts of Manipur.
• Security forces have been deployed in sensitive areas to prevent further escalation of violence.
• The incident highlights the fragile security situation and ongoing ethnic tensions in the state.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Arambai Tenggol is an armed Meitei organization operating in Manipur.
Imphal Airport is the primary airport serving the state capital of Manipur.
Manipur has been experiencing significant ethnic violence since 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of armed non-state actors like Arambai Tenggol in perpetuating conflict in Manipur.
Discuss the challenges faced by law enforcement in maintaining peace amidst ethnic strife and the arrest of influential figures.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government strategies in addressing the root causes of the Manipur violence.
Examine the broader implications of such unrest on regional stability and national security.
articles.imageDescription: Security forces patrolling a street in Imphal during protests following the arrest of an armed group leader.
articles.trivia: Arambai Tenggol was initially formed as a cultural organization before evolving into an armed group involved in the ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: Police Officer Killed in Naxal Bomb Attack in Chhattisgarh's Sukma District
articles.explainer:
• A police officer was tragically killed in a bomb attack carried out by Naxalites in Sukma district, Chhattisgarh.
• The incident occurred during an anti-Naxal operation in a remote forested area.
• Several other security personnel sustained injuries and have been evacuated for medical treatment.
• This attack highlights the persistent threat of left-wing extremism (LWE) in the Bastar region.
• Security forces have launched a search and combing operation to track down the perpetrators.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Sukma district is located in the Bastar division of Chhattisgarh, a known stronghold of Naxalites.
Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) is considered one of the biggest internal security threats to India.
The 'Red Corridor' refers to regions in central and eastern India affected by Naxalite-Maoist insurgency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the persistence of Naxalism in regions like Sukma despite sustained security operations.
Discuss the 'Samanvaya' strategy (security, development, and local governance) employed by the government to counter LWE.
Evaluate the challenges faced by security forces in anti-Naxal operations, including terrain, intelligence, and local support for extremists.
Suggest comprehensive measures beyond military action to address the socio-economic roots of Naxalism.
articles.imageDescription: Paramilitary forces conducting a search operation in a forested area of Sukma, Chhattisgarh.
articles.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

articles.headline: Fatal Train Accident in Mumbai Due to Overcrowding, Four Killed
articles.explainer:
• At least four individuals lost their lives and several others sustained injuries after falling from an overcrowded local train.
• The tragic incident occurred between Diva and Kopar railway stations in Mumbai, a heavily used commuter route.
• Initial reports suggest extreme overcrowding led to passengers losing their grip and falling onto the tracks.
• This incident highlights the persistent issue of inadequate infrastructure and capacity challenges in Mumbai's suburban rail network.
• Railway authorities have initiated an inquiry into the accident and announced compensation for the victims.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment
Infrastructure: Railways
prelimsPoints: Mumbai's suburban railway network is one of the busiest in the world.
Diva and Kopar are stations on the Central Line of the Mumbai Suburban Railway.
Railway safety is a critical aspect of public transportation in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to extreme overcrowding in Mumbai's local trains and its impact on public safety.
Analyze the challenges in upgrading and expanding urban railway infrastructure to meet the demands of a growing metropolitan population.
Suggest innovative solutions and policy interventions to mitigate risks associated with overcrowding in public transport systems.
Examine the role of urban planning and smart city initiatives in addressing infrastructure deficits in mega-cities like Mumbai.
articles.imageDescription: Crowded platform at a Mumbai suburban railway station with a local train arriving.
articles.trivia: The first passenger train in India ran from Bori Bunder (Mumbai) to Thane on April 16, 1853.

articles.headline: Singapore-Flagged Cargo Vessel Catches Fire Off Kerala Coast, Four Sailors Missing
articles.explainer:
• The Singapore-flagged cargo vessel MV Wan Hai 503 caught fire off the coast of Kerala.
• Four sailors from the vessel have been reported missing following the blaze.
• The Indian Coast Guard and other maritime agencies have launched a search and rescue operation.
• The incident raises concerns about maritime safety and environmental hazards from potential cargo spills.
• Investigations will likely focus on the cause of the fire and adherence to international safety protocols.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Environmental pollution and degradation
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Kerala has a significant coastline along the Arabian Sea.
The Indian Coast Guard is responsible for maritime search and rescue operations in India's Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
Singapore is a major global shipping hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and protocols involved in maritime search and rescue operations, especially for large cargo vessels.
Analyze the potential environmental and economic impacts of ship fires and hazardous cargo spills in coastal waters.
Evaluate India's preparedness and coordination mechanisms for responding to maritime disasters.
Examine the international regulations and conventions governing maritime safety and pollution prevention (e.g., SOLAS, MARPOL).
articles.imageDescription: A large cargo ship engulfed in flames at sea, with rescue vessels approaching.
articles.trivia: The International Maritime Organization (IMO), a specialized agency of the United Nations, is responsible for regulating shipping.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Data Privacy Rights in Landmark Digital India Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment reinforcing fundamental data privacy rights for Indian citizens.
• The ruling came in a case challenging the collection and usage of personal data by private entities and government agencies.
• It emphasized the need for robust data protection laws aligned with global best practices and the Puttaswamy judgment.
• The decision is expected to have wide-ranging implications for the ongoing Digital India initiatives and technology companies.
• Experts believe the ruling will accelerate the finalization and implementation of India's Data Protection Bill.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution in the K.S. Puttaswamy case (2017).
The Personal Data Protection Bill (PDPB) aims to provide for the protection of personal data of individuals.
Digital India is a flagship program of the Government of India with a vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and the significance of the latest Supreme Court ruling in the context of Digital India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security, economic growth, and individual privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of a robust data protection law on citizens' rights, government operations, and the technology sector in India.
Critically examine the provisions of the proposed Data Protection Bill and suggest measures for its effective implementation and enforcement.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with the Supreme Court of India building in the background, symbolizing justice and legal rulings.
articles.trivia: The Justice B. N. Srikrishna Committee was constituted by the government to draft India's data protection law.

articles.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Easing Inflation, Focus on Growth Revival
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision, keeping the repo rate unchanged.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though remaining within the RBI's comfort zone.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to supporting economic growth while remaining vigilant on inflationary pressures.
• Experts interpret this as a cautious stance, balancing the need for growth with price stability.
• Future policy actions will largely depend on evolving inflation trajectories and global economic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current economic scenario in India, considering inflation trends, growth prospects, and global headwinds.
Discuss the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in achieving its dual objectives of price stability and growth.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the MPC in making policy decisions, including supply-side shocks, global commodity prices, and fiscal policy stances.
Examine the concept of 'Inflation Targeting' in India and its implications for economic management.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, with financial charts overlaid, symbolizing economic policy.
articles.trivia: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly seen significant progress in recent rounds.
• Both sides expressed optimism about resolving key contentious issues, including market access for goods, services, and investment.
• A successful FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• This agreement is crucial for India's strategic economic engagement with one of the world's largest economic blocs.
• Discussions are ongoing, with a focus on intellectual property rights, sustainable development, and dispute resolution mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is pursuing FTAs with several key economic partners to enhance its global trade footprint.
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the major challenges and opportunities for India in negotiating comprehensive trade agreements with advanced economies like the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of an India-EU FTA on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, services, and agriculture.
Examine how such trade agreements align with India's broader foreign policy objectives and its vision for global economic integration.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union waving together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation and trade.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, after the US and China.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable.
• This advancement places India among a select few nations developing such sophisticated space technology.
• The successful test is a crucial step towards India's future manned space missions and commercial space ambitions.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology aims to reduce the cost of space access by reusing rocket components.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze how indigenization of advanced space technology contributes to national self-reliance and economic growth.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges associated with developing and deploying RLV systems for future space missions.
Examine India's vision for space exploration and its role in the global space economy, considering initiatives like Gaganyaan and commercial launches.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a controlled landing.
articles.trivia: The first experimental flight of ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) was successfully conducted in 2016.

articles.headline: New National Policy on Waste Management Launched to Boost Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a comprehensive new National Policy on Waste Management, focusing on circular economy principles.
• The policy aims to reduce waste generation, enhance waste segregation at source, and promote recycling and reuse.
• It introduces stringent regulations for plastic waste, e-waste, and construction & demolition waste.
• Financial incentives and technological interventions are proposed to encourage private sector participation in waste processing.
• This initiative is expected to significantly improve urban sanitation, public health, and environmental sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The concept of a 'circular economy' aims to eliminate waste and pollution, circulate products and materials, and regenerate nature.
Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (Clean India Mission) is a nationwide campaign by the Government of India to eliminate open defecation and improve solid waste management.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) holds producers responsible for the environmental impact of their products throughout their lifecycle.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the new National Policy on Waste Management and its potential to transform waste disposal practices in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective waste management strategies in urban and rural areas, including public participation and infrastructure deficits.
Evaluate the role of circular economy principles in achieving sustainable development goals and mitigating environmental pollution.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy reforms to enhance waste processing, resource recovery, and reduction of landfill burden in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the stages of a circular economy with waste being recycled and reused, emphasizing sustainability.
articles.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh tonnes of solid waste every day, with a significant portion remaining untreated.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana' for Holistic Tribal Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new flagship scheme, 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana', aimed at holistic development of tribal communities.
• The scheme focuses on improving access to education, healthcare, livelihood opportunities, and infrastructure in tribal areas.
• It adopts a multi-sectoral approach, integrating efforts from various ministries and state governments.
• Special emphasis is placed on preserving tribal culture, language, and traditional knowledge systems.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the developmental gap between tribal and non-tribal populations, promoting inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for the overall policy, planning and coordination of programmes for the development of Scheduled Tribes.
Article 46 of the Indian Constitution promotes the educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other weaker sections.
The Forest Rights Act, 2006, recognizes the rights of forest-dwelling tribal communities to forest resources.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic challenges faced by tribal communities in India and the rationale behind launching comprehensive development schemes like 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana'.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach adopted by the scheme and its potential to address issues related to health, education, and livelihood for tribal populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous tribal welfare programs and suggest measures to ensure better implementation and impact of new initiatives.
Examine the importance of preserving tribal cultural identity and traditional knowledge in the context of modern development and integration.
articles.imageDescription: A group of tribal children attending a school in a remote village, symbolizing educational development.
articles.trivia: India has the largest tribal population in the world, with over 700 recognized Scheduled Tribes.

articles.headline: PM Inaugurates Key Section of Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor, Boosts Regional Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Prime Minister inaugurated a crucial section of the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC), marking a major milestone.
• This section includes new industrial parks, multimodal logistics hubs, and advanced infrastructure facilities.
• The project aims to create a 'Smart Industrial City' along the corridor, attracting domestic and foreign investment.
• It is expected to significantly reduce transit times, lower logistics costs, and boost manufacturing capabilities.
• The DMIC is a flagship initiative under the National Industrial Corridor Development Programme (NICDP).
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Industrial policy.
prelimsPoints: The Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) is one of India's most ambitious infrastructure programs, aiming to develop industrial zones along a freight corridor.
The project is being implemented by the National Industrial Corridor Development Corporation (NICDC).
It is supported by financial and technical assistance from Japan.
mainsPoints: Analyze the concept and strategic importance of industrial corridors like DMIC in driving India's economic growth and industrialization.
Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of such mega-projects, including job creation, infrastructure development, and attracting foreign investment.
Evaluate the challenges involved in the planning, financing, and execution of large-scale infrastructure projects in India.
Examine how industrial corridors contribute to the 'Make in India' initiative and enhance India's position in global supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a newly developed industrial park with factories and well-laid roads, part of an industrial corridor.
articles.trivia: The DMIC project covers six states: Uttar Pradesh, National Capital Region of Delhi, Haryana, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Maharashtra.

articles.headline: India Participates in Quad Maritime Exercise, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security
articles.explainer:
• India joined the United States, Japan, and Australia in a major Quad maritime exercise in the Indo-Pacific region.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, information sharing, and joint operational capabilities among the navies.
• Key drills included anti-submarine warfare, air defense, and maritime interdiction operations.
• This collaboration underscores the Quad's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• The exercise sends a strong message regarding regional security and adherence to international maritime rules-based order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Quad is an informal strategic forum comprising India, Japan, Australia, and the United States.
The Indo-Pacific region is a geopolitical construct encompassing the Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean.
Malabar is a multilateral naval exercise involving the Quad member countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Quad grouping and its role in maintaining stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how India's participation in multilateral maritime exercises strengthens its naval capabilities and enhances interoperability with key partners.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of increased Quad cooperation, particularly in response to rising assertiveness in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in balancing its strategic autonomy with its commitments to regional security frameworks like the Quad.
articles.imageDescription: Warships from four different navies (India, US, Japan, Australia) sailing in formation during a maritime exercise.
articles.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Novel Indigenous Vaccine Development
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in the development of a novel indigenous vaccine for a prevalent infectious disease.
• The vaccine candidate showed promising results in preclinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This development underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and pharmaceutical innovation.
• The government has pledged accelerated support for clinical trials and mass production to address public health needs.
• The breakthrough is expected to enhance India's self-reliance in healthcare and global vaccine manufacturing leadership.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
prelimsPoints: India is often referred to as the 'Pharmacy of the World' due to its significant vaccine and pharmaceutical manufacturing capabilities.
The Department of Biotechnology (DBT) is a government department under the Ministry of Science and Technology.
Vaccines work by stimulating the body's immune system to produce antibodies against specific pathogens.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and its role in global health initiatives.
Analyze the ecosystem for biotechnological research and development in India, highlighting opportunities and challenges.
Evaluate how government policies and funding mechanisms can accelerate vaccine development from research to mass production and distribution.
Examine the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks crucial for the safe and effective development of new vaccines.
articles.imageDescription: Scientists in a laboratory examining vaccine samples under a microscope, symbolizing medical research.
articles.trivia: India produces around 60% of the world's vaccines, largely contributing to global immunization programs.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Meeting Held
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting on the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• The meeting assessed the readiness of states and educational institutions to adopt key reforms across school and higher education.
• Discussions focused on curriculum reforms, digital learning integration, teacher training, and vocational education expansion.
• Challenges in funding, infrastructure, and stakeholder coordination were also addressed.
• The government reiterated its commitment to transforming India's education landscape through NEP 2020.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level with 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) by 2030.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure in school education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the contemporary challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key reforms proposed by NEP 2020 across school and higher education, including curriculum, pedagogy, and assessment.
Evaluate the challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning federal coordination, resource allocation, and teacher preparedness.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to foster multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, and vocational skills to prepare India's youth for the 21st-century workforce.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning with digital tablets, symbolizing educational advancements.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 50 Years, New Census Data Shows Continued Growth
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's landmark wildlife conservation program, celebrated 50 years since its inception.
• New tiger census data released showcases a continued increase in India's tiger population, reaffirming conservation success.
• The report highlighted the challenges of habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
• India accounts for over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.
• The government reiterated its commitment to strengthening tiger reserves and combating wildlife crime.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India.
India conducts a tiger census every four years using the M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) methodology.
The tiger is the national animal of India and is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
mainsPoints: Analyze the success story of Project Tiger in India over the past 50 years and its contribution to global tiger conservation efforts.
Discuss the challenges that persist in tiger conservation, including habitat loss, human-wildlife conflict, and the illegal wildlife trade.
Evaluate the role of community participation and eco-tourism in sustainable wildlife management and conservation.
Examine government initiatives and policies aimed at protecting biodiversity and endangered species in India beyond Project Tiger.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage in a tiger reserve, symbolizing successful conservation.
articles.trivia: India has 54 tiger reserves spread across 18 states.

articles.headline: Centre-State Dialogue on Fiscal Decentralization and Resource Sharing Concludes
articles.explainer:
• A significant Centre-State dialogue on fiscal decentralization and resource sharing concluded with key recommendations.
• The discussions focused on enhancing financial autonomy for states and local bodies, aligning with cooperative federalism.
• Topics included GST compensation, vertical and horizontal devolution of taxes, and grants-in-aid.
• The dialogue aimed to address state concerns regarding fiscal space and funding for state-specific development priorities.
• Recommendations from the dialogue are expected to inform the upcoming recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body that determines the method and formula for distributing tax revenues between the Centre and states, and among states.
Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission.
GST (Goods and Services Tax) subsumed many indirect taxes at both central and state levels.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of fiscal federalism in India, particularly in the context of GST and the role of the Finance Commission.
Discuss the challenges faced by states in managing their finances and the demands for greater fiscal autonomy from the Centre.
Evaluate the principles of vertical and horizontal devolution of resources and their impact on balanced regional development.
Examine how mechanisms like Centre-State dialogues contribute to strengthening cooperative federalism and resolving fiscal disputes.
articles.imageDescription: A diagram illustrating the flow of funds between central and state governments, representing fiscal federalism.
articles.trivia: The 15th Finance Commission recommended a total devolution of 41% of the divisible pool of taxes to states for the period 2021-26.

articles.headline: New Initiative Launched to Digitize India's Rich Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a nationwide initiative to digitize India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
• The project aims to create a comprehensive digital repository of ancient manuscripts, artifacts, historical documents, and intangible heritage.
• It involves collaboration with various cultural institutions, museums, archives, and universities across the country.
• This initiative will ensure the preservation of fragile heritage, enhance accessibility for researchers, and promote cultural tourism.
• The digital platform will leverage AI and virtual reality to offer immersive experiences to global audiences.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: India is home to numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
The National Mission for Manuscripts (NMM) was launched to conserve and document Indian manuscripts.

Intangible Cultural Heritage includes traditions, oral expressions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, knowledge and practices concerning nature and the universe, and traditional craftsmanship.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for digitalization in preserving India's rich and diverse cultural heritage for future generations.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges in leveraging technology, such as AI and VR, to make cultural heritage accessible and engaging for a wider audience.
Evaluate the potential impact of cultural heritage digitalization on research, education, and cultural tourism in India.
Examine the role of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in funding and executing large-scale heritage preservation projects.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of an ancient Indian manuscript being viewed on a tablet, symbolizing heritage preservation through technology.
articles.trivia: The 'Memory of the World' Programme by UNESCO aims at safeguarding documentary heritage.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Reforms to Boost Agricultural Credit and Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a series of reforms aimed at improving access to agricultural credit for farmers.
• New policies focus on streamlining loan procedures, reducing interest rates, and expanding collateral-free lending.
• The reforms also include measures to promote digital transactions and financial literacy among rural populations.
• This initiative is part of a broader strategy to double farmer incomes and ensure agricultural sustainability.
• Emphasis is placed on supporting small and marginal farmers, and integrating technology for efficient credit delivery.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is the apex development bank for agriculture and rural development in India.
Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme aims to provide adequate and timely credit support to farmers.
Priority Sector Lending (PSL) mandates commercial banks to lend a certain portion of their loans to specific sectors, including agriculture.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical role of agricultural credit in enhancing farmer income and ensuring food security in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by farmers in accessing institutional credit and the impact of these challenges on agricultural productivity.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government reforms and schemes aimed at improving agricultural credit delivery and financial inclusion in rural areas.
Examine how technology and digital platforms can be leveraged to streamline credit processes and reduce the burden on small and marginal farmers.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with a tractor, symbolizing modern agricultural practices and credit support.
articles.trivia: The target for agricultural credit in India for the financial year 2024-25 was set at over ₹20 lakh crore.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Global Climate Finance at International Summit
articles.explainer:
• India participated actively in a major international summit on climate finance, advocating for equitable burden-sharing.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for developed nations to meet their climate finance commitments to developing countries.
• Discussions focused on innovative financing mechanisms, technology transfer, and capacity building for climate action.
• India highlighted its own ambitious climate targets and investments in renewable energy and adaptation measures.
• The summit aimed to build consensus on a new collective quantified goal for climate finance post-2025.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Environmental security and climate change.
prelimsPoints: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2°C, preferably to 1.5°C, compared to pre-industrial levels.
Developed countries committed to mobilizing $100 billion per year in climate finance for developing countries by 2020.
India's Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) includes targets for emissions intensity reduction and non-fossil fuel electricity capacity.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's position on climate finance and its advocacy for equitable burden-sharing between developed and developing nations.
Discuss the challenges in mobilizing and disbursing adequate climate finance to meet global climate action goals.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing climate finance mechanisms and suggest reforms for a more transparent and accessible system.
Examine the concept of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in the context of international climate negotiations.
articles.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by hands, symbolizing global cooperation on climate change and finance.
articles.trivia: The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a fund established within the framework of the UNFCCC to assist developing countries in adaptation and mitigation practices.

articles.headline: Pilot Project for Advanced Urban Air Quality Monitoring Launched in Metro Cities
articles.explainer:
• A pilot project for advanced urban air quality monitoring was launched in several major Indian metro cities.
• The initiative deploys a network of high-resolution sensors and AI-driven analytics for real-time pollution data.
• It aims to provide granular data on particulate matter, ozone, and other pollutants to inform policy decisions.
• The project seeks to enhance public awareness and enable targeted interventions for pollution control.
• This is a step towards implementing the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The Air Quality Index (AQI) is a tool for effective communication of air quality status to people.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) aims to reduce particulate matter concentration by 20-30% by 2024 (from 2017 levels).
The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of urban air pollution in India and its implications for public health and environmental sustainability.
Analyze how advanced air quality monitoring technologies can enhance data accuracy and inform effective pollution control strategies.
Evaluate the objectives and implementation challenges of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) in achieving its targets.
Examine the role of citizen science and public engagement in addressing air pollution and promoting sustainable urban living.
articles.imageDescription: A digital display showing real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) readings in a city, with a hazy skyline in the background.
articles.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the world's most polluted capital cities, especially during winter months.

highlights: Manipur witnesses fresh unrest after the arrest of Arambai Tenggol leader, Asem Kanan Singh, at Imphal Airport.
A police officer is killed in a Naxal bomb attack in Sukma district, Chhattisgarh, highlighting persistent LWE threat.
Supreme Court delivers a landmark ruling reinforcing data privacy rights, with significant implications for Digital India initiatives.
ISRO successfully tests its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, a step towards cost-effective space access.
India participates in a major Quad maritime exercise, reaffirming commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent events in Manipur, consider the following statements:
1. Arambai Tenggol is an armed Meitei group operating in Manipur.
2. The ongoing ethnic violence in Manipur primarily involves conflicts between the Meitei and Naga communities.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Arambai Tenggol is indeed an armed Meitei group. Statement 2 is incorrect. The ongoing ethnic violence in Manipur primarily involves conflicts between the Meitei and Kuki-Zo communities, not Naga communities in this context.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India, constituted under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's space program:
1. The Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
2. Gaganyaan is India's first mission to send humans to Mars.
3. ISRO has successfully tested RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) in the past.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. RLV technology aims to make space launches more cost-effective by reusing rocket components. Statement 2 is incorrect. Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission to Low Earth Orbit, not Mars. Statement 3 is correct. ISRO successfully conducted the first experimental flight of RLV-TD in 2016.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following international agreements is primarily concerned with establishing rules and standards for maritime safety?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) is an international maritime treaty which sets minimum safety standards in the construction, equipment and operation of merchant ships. MARPOL is for marine pollution, Basel for hazardous waste, and Kyoto Protocol for climate change.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to Project Tiger, consider the following statements:
1. Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
2. India accounts for over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
3. The tiger is listed as 'Critically Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Project Tiger was launched in 1973. Statement 2 is correct. India indeed hosts over 70% of the world's wild tiger population. Statement 3 is incorrect. The tiger is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List, not 'Critically Endangered'.

mainsMocks.question: The arrest of key figures from armed non-state actors often leads to further instability, as seen in the recent Manipur violence. Critically analyze the challenges involved in addressing internal security threats posed by such groups, and suggest comprehensive strategies for effective conflict resolution and peacebuilding in affected regions.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Linkages between development and spread of extremism; Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges; Internal security challenges.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the context of Manipur violence and the role of armed groups. Discuss challenges like local support, cross-border linkages, socio-economic grievances, and law enforcement limitations. Propose multi-pronged strategies: kinetic operations with intelligence, socio-economic development, good governance, community engagement, de-radicalization, and rehabilitation programs, and political dialogue.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reinforcement of data privacy rights is crucial in the era of Digital India. Discuss the constitutional underpinnings of the right to privacy in India and evaluate the implications of strong data protection laws for individual citizens, government services, and the technology sector.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) establishing privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21. Explain the constitutional basis and its evolution. Then, discuss implications: empowering citizens, ensuring accountability in government data handling (e.g., Aadhaar), and creating a regulatory framework for tech companies (data localization, consent). Highlight challenges in implementation and balancing privacy with national security.

mainsMocks.question: India's commitment to climate action is evident in its ambitious targets and its advocacy for equitable global climate finance. Examine the concept of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in international climate negotiations and critically assess its relevance in achieving global climate goals, particularly concerning finance and technology transfer.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Environmental security and climate change. GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Define CBDR-RC as a principle of international environmental law acknowledging historical emissions and varying capacities. Discuss its origins and significance for developing countries like India. Analyze how it frames demands for climate finance and technology transfer from developed nations. Critically assess its effectiveness: successes in past agreements, but also challenges in current negotiations, particularly with developed nations' reluctance to meet commitments. Conclude on its continued relevance for climate justice.

quickBytes.text: Arambai Tenggol is a Meitei armed group involved in the Manipur violence.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's repo rate.
The Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) aims to reduce space launch costs.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for tiger conservation.
The Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) is a major infrastructure project supported by Japan.
The Quad comprises India, USA, Japan, and Australia.
NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
The Finance Commission is a constitutional body under Article 280.
The 'Pharmacy of the World' refers to India's vaccine and pharmaceutical manufacturing.

GS1.headline: Fatal Train Accident in Mumbai Due to Overcrowding, Four Killed
GS1.explainer:
• At least four individuals lost their lives and several others sustained injuries after falling from an overcrowded local train.
• The tragic incident occurred between Diva and Kopar railway stations in Mumbai, a heavily used commuter route.
• Initial reports suggest extreme overcrowding led to passengers losing their grip and falling onto the tracks.
• This incident highlights the persistent issue of inadequate infrastructure and capacity challenges in Mumbai's suburban rail network.
• Railway authorities have initiated an inquiry into the accident and announced compensation for the victims.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment
Infrastructure: Railways
prelimsPoints: Mumbai's suburban railway network is one of the busiest in the world.
Diva and Kopar are stations on the Central Line of the Mumbai Suburban Railway.
Railway safety is a critical aspect of public transportation in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors contributing to extreme overcrowding in Mumbai's local trains and its impact on public safety.
Analyze the challenges in upgrading and expanding urban railway infrastructure to meet the demands of a growing metropolitan population.
Suggest innovative solutions and policy interventions to mitigate risks associated with overcrowding in public transport systems.
Examine the role of urban planning and smart city initiatives in addressing infrastructure deficits in mega-cities like Mumbai.
GS1.imageDescription: Crowded platform at a Mumbai suburban railway station with a local train arriving.
GS1.trivia: The first passenger train in India ran from Bori Bunder (Mumbai) to Thane on April 16, 1853.

GS1.headline: New Initiative Launched to Digitize India's Rich Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a nationwide initiative to digitize India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
• The project aims to create a comprehensive digital repository of ancient manuscripts, artifacts, historical documents, and intangible heritage.
• It involves collaboration with various cultural institutions, museums, archives, and universities across the country.
• This initiative will ensure the preservation of fragile heritage, enhance accessibility for researchers, and promote cultural tourism.
• The digital platform will leverage AI and virtual reality to offer immersive experiences to global audiences.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: India is home to numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
The National Mission for Manuscripts (NMM) was launched to conserve and document Indian manuscripts.

Intangible Cultural Heritage includes traditions, oral expressions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, knowledge and practices concerning nature and the universe, and traditional craftsmanship.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for digitalization in preserving India's rich and diverse cultural heritage for future generations.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges in leveraging technology, such as AI and VR, to make cultural heritage accessible and engaging for a wider audience.
Evaluate the potential impact of cultural heritage digitalization on research, education, and cultural tourism in India.
Examine the role of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in funding and executing large-scale heritage preservation projects.
GS1.imageDescription: A digital representation of an ancient Indian manuscript being viewed on a tablet, symbolizing heritage preservation through technology.
GS1.trivia: The 'Memory of the World' Programme by UNESCO aims at safeguarding documentary heritage.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Data Privacy Rights in Landmark Digital India Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment reinforcing fundamental data privacy rights for Indian citizens.
• The ruling came in a case challenging the collection and usage of personal data by private entities and government agencies.
• It emphasized the need for robust data protection laws aligned with global best practices and the Puttaswamy judgment.
• The decision is expected to have wide-ranging implications for the ongoing Digital India initiatives and technology companies.
• Experts believe the ruling will accelerate the finalization and implementation of India's Data Protection Bill.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution in the K.S. Puttaswamy case (2017).
The Personal Data Protection Bill (PDPB) aims to provide for the protection of personal data of individuals.
Digital India is a flagship program of the Government of India with a vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and the significance of the latest Supreme Court ruling in the context of Digital India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security, economic growth, and individual privacy rights in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of a robust data protection law on citizens' rights, government operations, and the technology sector in India.
Critically examine the provisions of the proposed Data Protection Bill and suggest measures for its effective implementation and enforcement.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with the Supreme Court of India building in the background, symbolizing justice and legal rulings.
GS2.trivia: The Justice B. N. Srikrishna Committee was constituted by the government to draft India's data protection law.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Positive Momentum
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly seen significant progress in recent rounds.
• Both sides expressed optimism about resolving key contentious issues, including market access for goods, services, and investment.
• A successful FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• This agreement is crucial for India's strategic economic engagement with one of the world's largest economic blocs.
• Discussions are ongoing, with a focus on intellectual property rights, sustainable development, and dispute resolution mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states.
India is pursuing FTAs with several key economic partners to enhance its global trade footprint.
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union.
Discuss the major challenges and opportunities for India in negotiating comprehensive trade agreements with advanced economies like the EU.
Evaluate the potential impact of an India-EU FTA on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, services, and agriculture.
Examine how such trade agreements align with India's broader foreign policy objectives and its vision for global economic integration.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union waving together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation and trade.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, after the US and China.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana' for Holistic Tribal Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new flagship scheme, 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana', aimed at holistic development of tribal communities.
• The scheme focuses on improving access to education, healthcare, livelihood opportunities, and infrastructure in tribal areas.
• It adopts a multi-sectoral approach, integrating efforts from various ministries and state governments.
• Special emphasis is placed on preserving tribal culture, language, and traditional knowledge systems.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the developmental gap between tribal and non-tribal populations, promoting inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Tribal Affairs is the nodal ministry for the overall policy, planning and coordination of programmes for the development of Scheduled Tribes.
Article 46 of the Indian Constitution promotes the educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other weaker sections.
The Forest Rights Act, 2006, recognizes the rights of forest-dwelling tribal communities to forest resources.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic challenges faced by tribal communities in India and the rationale behind launching comprehensive development schemes like 'Jan Jati Vikas Yojana'.
Discuss the multi-sectoral approach adopted by the scheme and its potential to address issues related to health, education, and livelihood for tribal populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of previous tribal welfare programs and suggest measures to ensure better implementation and impact of new initiatives.
Examine the importance of preserving tribal cultural identity and traditional knowledge in the context of modern development and integration.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of tribal children attending a school in a remote village, symbolizing educational development.
GS2.trivia: India has the largest tribal population in the world, with over 700 recognized Scheduled Tribes.

GS2.headline: India Participates in Quad Maritime Exercise, Reinforcing Indo-Pacific Security
GS2.explainer:
• India joined the United States, Japan, and Australia in a major Quad maritime exercise in the Indo-Pacific region.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, information sharing, and joint operational capabilities among the navies.
• Key drills included anti-submarine warfare, air defense, and maritime interdiction operations.
• This collaboration underscores the Quad's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• The exercise sends a strong message regarding regional security and adherence to international maritime rules-based order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Quad is an informal strategic forum comprising India, Japan, Australia, and the United States.
The Indo-Pacific region is a geopolitical construct encompassing the Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean.
Malabar is a multilateral naval exercise involving the Quad member countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the Quad grouping and its role in maintaining stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how India's participation in multilateral maritime exercises strengthens its naval capabilities and enhances interoperability with key partners.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of increased Quad cooperation, particularly in response to rising assertiveness in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in balancing its strategic autonomy with its commitments to regional security frameworks like the Quad.
GS2.imageDescription: Warships from four different navies (India, US, Japan, Australia) sailing in formation during a maritime exercise.
GS2.trivia: The idea of the Quad was first mooted by Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe in 2007.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Meeting Held
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting on the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• The meeting assessed the readiness of states and educational institutions to adopt key reforms across school and higher education.
• Discussions focused on curriculum reforms, digital learning integration, teacher training, and vocational education expansion.
• Challenges in funding, infrastructure, and stakeholder coordination were also addressed.
• The government reiterated its commitment to transforming India's education landscape through NEP 2020.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level with 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) by 2030.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure in school education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in addressing the contemporary challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key reforms proposed by NEP 2020 across school and higher education, including curriculum, pedagogy, and assessment.
Evaluate the challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning federal coordination, resource allocation, and teacher preparedness.
Examine how NEP 2020 aims to foster multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, and vocational skills to prepare India's youth for the 21st-century workforce.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning with digital tablets, symbolizing educational advancements.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

GS2.headline: Centre-State Dialogue on Fiscal Decentralization and Resource Sharing Concludes
GS2.explainer:
• A significant Centre-State dialogue on fiscal decentralization and resource sharing concluded with key recommendations.
• The discussions focused on enhancing financial autonomy for states and local bodies, aligning with cooperative federalism.
• Topics included GST compensation, vertical and horizontal devolution of taxes, and grants-in-aid.
• The dialogue aimed to address state concerns regarding fiscal space and funding for state-specific development priorities.
• Recommendations from the dialogue are expected to inform the upcoming recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body that determines the method and formula for distributing tax revenues between the Centre and states, and among states.
Article 280 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission.
GST (Goods and Services Tax) subsumed many indirect taxes at both central and state levels.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving dynamics of fiscal federalism in India, particularly in the context of GST and the role of the Finance Commission.
Discuss the challenges faced by states in managing their finances and the demands for greater fiscal autonomy from the Centre.
Evaluate the principles of vertical and horizontal devolution of resources and their impact on balanced regional development.
Examine how mechanisms like Centre-State dialogues contribute to strengthening cooperative federalism and resolving fiscal disputes.
GS2.imageDescription: A diagram illustrating the flow of funds between central and state governments, representing fiscal federalism.
GS2.trivia: The 15th Finance Commission recommended a total devolution of 41% of the divisible pool of taxes to states for the period 2021-26.

GS3.headline: Manipur Unrest Escalates After Arrest of Arambai Tenggol Leader and Associates
GS3.explainer:
• Asem Kanan Singh, leader of the armed Meitei group Arambai Tenggol, was arrested at Imphal Airport.
• Four other officials associated with the group were also apprehended during the operation.
• The arrests immediately triggered widespread unrest and protests across various parts of Manipur.
• Security forces have been deployed in sensitive areas to prevent further escalation of violence.
• The incident highlights the fragile security situation and ongoing ethnic tensions in the state.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Arambai Tenggol is an armed Meitei organization operating in Manipur.
Imphal Airport is the primary airport serving the state capital of Manipur.
Manipur has been experiencing significant ethnic violence since 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of armed non-state actors like Arambai Tenggol in perpetuating conflict in Manipur.
Discuss the challenges faced by law enforcement in maintaining peace amidst ethnic strife and the arrest of influential figures.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current government strategies in addressing the root causes of the Manipur violence.
Examine the broader implications of such unrest on regional stability and national security.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces patrolling a street in Imphal during protests following the arrest of an armed group leader.
GS3.trivia: Arambai Tenggol was initially formed as a cultural organization before evolving into an armed group involved in the ongoing conflict.

GS3.headline: Police Officer Killed in Naxal Bomb Attack in Chhattisgarh's Sukma District
GS3.explainer:
• A police officer was tragically killed in a bomb attack carried out by Naxalites in Sukma district, Chhattisgarh.
• The incident occurred during an anti-Naxal operation in a remote forested area.
• Several other security personnel sustained injuries and have been evacuated for medical treatment.
• This attack highlights the persistent threat of left-wing extremism (LWE) in the Bastar region.
• Security forces have launched a search and combing operation to track down the perpetrators.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Sukma district is located in the Bastar division of Chhattisgarh, a known stronghold of Naxalites.
Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) is considered one of the biggest internal security threats to India.
The 'Red Corridor' refers to regions in central and eastern India affected by Naxalite-Maoist insurgency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the persistence of Naxalism in regions like Sukma despite sustained security operations.
Discuss the 'Samanvaya' strategy (security, development, and local governance) employed by the government to counter LWE.
Evaluate the challenges faced by security forces in anti-Naxal operations, including terrain, intelligence, and local support for extremists.
Suggest comprehensive measures beyond military action to address the socio-economic roots of Naxalism.
GS3.imageDescription: Paramilitary forces conducting a search operation in a forested area of Sukma, Chhattisgarh.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

GS3.headline: Singapore-Flagged Cargo Vessel Catches Fire Off Kerala Coast, Four Sailors Missing
GS3.explainer:
• The Singapore-flagged cargo vessel MV Wan Hai 503 caught fire off the coast of Kerala.
• Four sailors from the vessel have been reported missing following the blaze.
• The Indian Coast Guard and other maritime agencies have launched a search and rescue operation.
• The incident raises concerns about maritime safety and environmental hazards from potential cargo spills.
• Investigations will likely focus on the cause of the fire and adherence to international safety protocols.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Environmental pollution and degradation
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Kerala has a significant coastline along the Arabian Sea.
The Indian Coast Guard is responsible for maritime search and rescue operations in India's Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
Singapore is a major global shipping hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and protocols involved in maritime search and rescue operations, especially for large cargo vessels.
Analyze the potential environmental and economic impacts of ship fires and hazardous cargo spills in coastal waters.
Evaluate India's preparedness and coordination mechanisms for responding to maritime disasters.
Examine the international regulations and conventions governing maritime safety and pollution prevention (e.g., SOLAS, MARPOL).
GS3.imageDescription: A large cargo ship engulfed in flames at sea, with rescue vessels approaching.
GS3.trivia: The International Maritime Organization (IMO), a specialized agency of the United Nations, is responsible for regulating shipping.

GS3.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Easing Inflation, Focus on Growth Revival
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision, keeping the repo rate unchanged.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, though remaining within the RBI's comfort zone.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to supporting economic growth while remaining vigilant on inflationary pressures.
• Experts interpret this as a cautious stance, balancing the need for growth with price stability.
• Future policy actions will largely depend on evolving inflation trajectories and global economic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current economic scenario in India, considering inflation trends, growth prospects, and global headwinds.
Discuss the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in achieving its dual objectives of price stability and growth.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the MPC in making policy decisions, including supply-side shocks, global commodity prices, and fiscal policy stances.
Examine the concept of 'Inflation Targeting' in India and its implications for economic management.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, with financial charts overlaid, symbolizing economic policy.
GS3.trivia: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• This test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable.
• This advancement places India among a select few nations developing such sophisticated space technology.
• The successful test is a crucial step towards India's future manned space missions and commercial space ambitions.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology aims to reduce the cost of space access by reusing rocket components.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze how indigenization of advanced space technology contributes to national self-reliance and economic growth.
Evaluate the potential benefits and challenges associated with developing and deploying RLV systems for future space missions.
Examine India's vision for space exploration and its role in the global space economy, considering initiatives like Gaganyaan and commercial launches.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a controlled landing.
GS3.trivia: The first experimental flight of ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) was successfully conducted in 2016.

GS3.headline: New National Policy on Waste Management Launched to Boost Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a comprehensive new National Policy on Waste Management, focusing on circular economy principles.
• The policy aims to reduce waste generation, enhance waste segregation at source, and promote recycling and reuse.
• It introduces stringent regulations for plastic waste, e-waste, and construction & demolition waste.
• Financial incentives and technological interventions are proposed to encourage private sector participation in waste processing.
• This initiative is expected to significantly improve urban sanitation, public health, and environmental sustainability.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The concept of a 'circular economy' aims to eliminate waste and pollution, circulate products and materials, and regenerate nature.
Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (Clean India Mission) is a nationwide campaign by the Government of India to eliminate open defecation and improve solid waste management.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) holds producers responsible for the environmental impact of their products throughout their lifecycle.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key features and objectives of the new National Policy on Waste Management and its potential to transform waste disposal practices in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective waste management strategies in urban and rural areas, including public participation and infrastructure deficits.
Evaluate the role of circular economy principles in achieving sustainable development goals and mitigating environmental pollution.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy reforms to enhance waste processing, resource recovery, and reduction of landfill burden in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the stages of a circular economy with waste being recycled and reused, emphasizing sustainability.
GS3.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh tonnes of solid waste every day, with a significant portion remaining untreated.

GS3.headline: PM Inaugurates Key Section of Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor, Boosts Regional Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Prime Minister inaugurated a crucial section of the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC), marking a major milestone.
• This section includes new industrial parks, multimodal logistics hubs, and advanced infrastructure facilities.
• The project aims to create a 'Smart Industrial City' along the corridor, attracting domestic and foreign investment.
• It is expected to significantly reduce transit times, lower logistics costs, and boost manufacturing capabilities.
• The DMIC is a flagship initiative under the National Industrial Corridor Development Programme (NICDP).
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Industrial policy.
prelimsPoints: The Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) is one of India's most ambitious infrastructure programs, aiming to develop industrial zones along a freight corridor.
The project is being implemented by the National Industrial Corridor Development Corporation (NICDC).
It is supported by financial and technical assistance from Japan.
